title: Pine and Plain Rectangle Coffee Table
summary: A coffee table built almost entirely from pine project panels — legs, top, and shelf cut from wide panel stock, tied together by a 1x12 center support and just two boards of dimensional lumber.
description:
Instead of the usual 2x4 or 4x4 legs, this table's two legs are cut directly from a wide 3/4" pine project panel — flat panel stock standing in for a leg, which keeps the whole build to just a handful of large, simple parts rather than a frame of many small ones. The top is a single 48" project panel, and the shelf below is another panel cut to length.

A 1x12 center support runs the length of the table underneath, with two shorter 1x12 dividers **cut to fit your own assembled base** rather than a fixed length, since panel thickness can vary slightly board to board. Two 1x3 bottom supports tie the legs together at floor level and keep the shelf from sagging.

Assemble with pocket holes if you have a jig, or fall back to self-tapping screws or brad nails and glue — the plan is explicit that any of the three joinery methods works for a build this simple.

materials.name: Pocket hole screws, 1-1/4", or self-tapping screws, 2", or brad nails, 1-1/4"
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Measure and cut to fit your assembled base.

steps.title: Cut the legs and top
steps.body: Cut the two panel legs and the top panel to size.

steps.title: Attach the center support
steps.body: Attach the 1x12 center support between the two legs.
materials: Pocket hole screws, 1-1/4", or self-tapping screws, 2", or brad nails, 1-1/4"

steps.title: Fit the dividers
steps.body: Measure the assembled base and cut the two dividers to fit, then attach.
materials: Pocket hole screws, 1-1/4", or self-tapping screws, 2", or brad nails, 1-1/4"

steps.title: Add the shelf and bottom supports
steps.body: Attach the shelf panel and the two bottom supports between the legs near floor level.
materials: Pocket hole screws, 1-1/4", or self-tapping screws, 2", or brad nails, 1-1/4"

steps.title: Attach the top
steps.body: Attach the top panel to the leg assembly.
materials: Pocket hole screws, 1-1/4", or self-tapping screws, 2", or brad nails, 1-1/4"

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Pine and plain rectangle coffee table with panel legs
